  "findings": [
    "Sling angle is below the recommended minimum of 30 degrees",
    "Tension in each sling leg (3,549.3 lbs) exceeds the tagged Working Load Limit (3,500 lbs)"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the current rigging plan",
    "Increase the sling angle to at least 45 or 60 degrees by using longer slings or a spreader bar",
    "Ensure the tension on each leg is well below its rated Working Load Limit before proceeding"
  ],
  "rationale": "The tension in each leg is calculated as (Load / 2) / sin(angle) = (3,000 / 2) / sin(25 degrees) = 1,500 / 0.4226 = 3,549.3 lbs. This exceeds the tagged vertical WLL of 3,500 lbs. Additionally, rigging at a horizontal angle of less than 30 degrees is extremely hazardous and generally prohibited by ASME B30.9 and OSHA standards without special engineering approval.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
